title: habranthus hesperius
intro: Habranthus hesperius, also known as the golden daffodil, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Habranthus hesperius include its ability to relieve coughs and congestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine in the regions where it is native.
